event: Madonna and Child enthroned with saints
symbols: elevated throne — the Virgin's exalted role as Queen of Heaven and Theotokos
infant Christ on Mary's lap — the Incarnation, God taking human flesh
armored knight with red-cross banner — the soldier of Christ and defender of the faith
Franciscan friar in brown habit — poverty, humility, and devotion following Saint Francis
coat of arms on throne — patronage and earthly devotion to the heavenly court
checkered floor — the ordered space of sacred encounter
landscape with castle — the earthly realm under the protection of the Madonna
description: A sacra conversazione composition with the Virgin Mary seated on a tall, ornate green throne, holding the infant Jesus on her lap. She wears a red robe and blue mantle. The throne is elevated above a checkered floor and bears a coat of arms. To the left stands a knight in full plate armor holding a tall banner with a red cross. To the right stands a friar in a brown Franciscan habit with hands clasped in prayer. Behind the figures, a gentle landscape with a castle and distant hills is visible under a clear sky.
theological_description: The sacra conversazione — sacred conversation — is a devotional format showing the Madonna and Child with saints, representing the communion of the heavenly court. The enthroned Virgin with Christ embodies the theology of the Incarnation: Mary as Theotokos, the God-bearer, holding the divine Word made flesh (John 1:14). Her elevated position reflects the Magnificat's proclamation that 'all generations will call me blessed' (Luke 1:48). The armored saint bearing a cross-emblazoned banner represents the Church Militant, the faithful who wage spiritual warfare under the banner of the cross (Ephesians 6:10-17). The Franciscan friar embodies the evangelical counsels of poverty and humility, following Christ's call to 'take up your cross' (Matthew 16:24). Together, the martial and contemplative figures flanking the throne represent the two modes of Christian discipleship — the active and contemplative life — both oriented toward Christ through Mary's mediation.

scripture_refs.reason: The Word became flesh — the Incarnation theology at the heart of every Madonna and Child image
The Magnificat and Mary's exaltation, reflected in her enthroned position
The armor of God and spiritual warfare, represented by the armored knight saint
Taking up the cross and following Christ, embodied by the Franciscan friar

_meta.title: Madonna and Child with Saints Liberale and Francis (The Castelfranco Madonna)
